title: U.S.–Israel war with Iran enters fifth day
summary: The conflict between the United States, Israel, and Iran has escalated rapidly. A U.S. submarine sank an Iranian warship near Sri Lanka while Iran launched missiles and drones toward regional targets. Fighting is spreading across the Middle East, raising fears of a wider regional war.
why_it_matters: The conflict could disrupt global energy supplies, shipping routes in the Persian Gulf, and international security. It also raises concerns about civilian casualties and the possibility of more countries getting involved.

sources.title: Reuters: U.S. sinks Iranian warship as conflict widens
Guardian: Israel launches new wave of strikes as war escalates

title: World leaders split on how to respond to the Iran war
summary: Countries across Europe and the Middle East are divided on how to respond to the conflict between Iran, Israel, and the United States. Some governments support Israel’s efforts to weaken Iran’s military capabilities, while others warn the strikes could violate international law or risk a wider war.
why_it_matters: International support or opposition could determine whether the conflict escalates further or moves toward diplomacy.

sources.title: Al Jazeera: Europe divided on response to U.S.–Israel strikes

title: Congress debates whether Trump can continue the Iran war
summary: Members of Congress are debating whether President Trump has the authority to continue military strikes against Iran without formal approval from lawmakers. Some Republicans support the operation as necessary for national security, while many Democrats argue Congress must authorize any extended war.
why_it_matters: The decision could determine how long the United States remains involved in the conflict and how much power the president has to launch military action.

sources.title: Reuters: Congress considers war powers resolutions on Iran

title: Iran conflict reshapes U.S. politics and Trump’s support
summary: The war with Iran is becoming a major political issue in the United States. Supporters say the strikes show strength against Iran’s military and nuclear ambitions, while critics worry the conflict could spiral into a long and costly war.
why_it_matters: Public opinion about the war could affect elections, U.S. foreign policy, and how Congress responds to future military actions.

sources.title: Reuters: Iran crisis tests Trump’s political support

title: First 2026 midterm primaries reveal political trends
summary: The first primaries of the 2026 U.S. midterm election season are beginning. Early results are giving clues about which issues are motivating voters and which candidates may shape the balance of power in Congress.
why_it_matters: The midterm elections will determine whether Republicans or Democrats control the House and Senate, affecting future laws, budgets, and investigations.

sources.title: Reuters: Key takeaways from first 2026 midterm primaries
